steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
The word but sets up a contrast: until recently, Theia had a certain status, but now researchers claim to have identified actual pieces of it inside Earth.

The blank must describe Theia before physical evidence existed — that is, when it was just an idea in a theory.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Notional means "existing as an idea or concept rather than as something real." That fits perfectly: until pieces were found, Theia was only a theoretical construct.

Spurious (false) and desultory (lacking purpose) don't fit; veritable (genuine) is the opposite of what's needed.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
